proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Lower Extremity Trauma Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite pelvis-to-foot model linking pelvic ring, knee, leg, and hindfoot structures for trauma neighborhoods.
Bony pelvic ring formed by ilium, ischium, pubis, and sacrum.
Posterior pelvic anchor articulating with ilia at the sacroiliac joints.
Posterior pelvic articulation between sacrum and ilium; key pelvic ring stability structure.
Anterior midline pelvic articulation; disruption indicates pelvic ring instability.
Lateral pelvic ilium region relevant to acetabular column and pelvic ring injuries.
Hip socket formed by ilium, ischium, and pubis; injured in acetabular fractures.
Femoral shaft between proximal and distal metaphyseal regions.
Distal femoral metaphysis and condylar region forming the proximal knee.
Proximal tibial articular surface bearing medial and lateral condylar load.
Sesamoid bone within the extensor mechanism at the anterior knee.
Quadriceps-patella-tibial tubercle linkage enabling knee extension.
Primary knee stabilizer commonly injured with tibial plateau and knee trauma.
Posterior knee stabilizer relevant in high-energy knee and plateau injuries.
Major knee-region artery at risk in tibial plateau, distal femur, and knee dislocation trauma.
Lateral knee nerve at risk in tibial plateau and proximal fibular injuries.
Tibiotalar and subtalar articular bone with limited vascular supply.
Hindfoot bone articulating with talus at the subtalar joint.
Talocalcaneal articulation governing hindfoot alignment after calcaneus and talus injuries.
Tarsometatarsal articulation complex; instability defines Lisfranc injury.
Central foot region including navicular, cuneiforms, and cuboid linking hindfoot to forefoot.
Plantar fascia and soft-tissue envelope relevant to calcaneus fracture swelling and wound risk.
High-energy pelvic ring disruption with instability, hemorrhage, and resuscitation priorities.
Mechanism-based pelvic ring injury classification (APC, LC, VS).
Emergency circumferential compression to reduce pelvic volume and tamponade hemorrhage.
Anterior frame stabilization for unstable pelvic ring injuries during resuscitation or definitive care.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Unstable Pelvic Ring Pattern

metadata.description: Radiographic pattern with anterior and/or posterior ring disruption indicating mechanical instability.
Life-threatening retroperitoneal bleeding from pelvic ring and venous plexus injury.

metadata.claim_text: Pelvic ring injuries are evaluated as anterior and posterior ring disruptions where instability pattern drives binder, ex-fix, and hemorrhage control priorities.
Pelvic binder application temporizes hemorrhage but does not replace assessment of unstable ring patterns requiring definitive stabilization.
Focusing only on pubic symphysis widening without evaluating sacroiliac disruption in high-energy pelvic trauma.
State mechanism, hemodynamic status, binder placement, ring stability assessment, transfusion needs, and ex-fix versus definitive OR plan.
Sacroiliac joint and pubic symphysis integrity define anterior versus posterior pelvic ring stability contributions.

metadata.trigger: Unstable pelvic ring pattern with mechanical instability after resuscitation
metadata.action: Apply pelvic binder if not in place; discuss anterior external fixation or definitive stabilization with trauma team and attending

metadata.trigger: Hemodynamically unstable patient with pelvic ring injury and suspected pelvic hemorrhage
metadata.action: Activate massive transfusion pathway, pelvic binder, angioembolization discussion, and expedited ring stabilization per attending

decisions.reviewerDecisionRequired: Confirm clinical accuracy and atomicity of claim: claim-pri-ring-instability
